name: Charlie Kirk
summary: Charlie Kirk has consistently demonstrated a pattern of behavior that involves spreading false and misleading information, promoting conspiracy theories, and making inflammatory and discriminatory statements about various groups, including racial and ethnic minorities, women, and members of the LGBTQ+ community. He has also shown a willingness to engage in divisive and hateful rhetoric, often using his platform to attack and demean individuals and groups who disagree with him. Additionally, Kirk has been involved in efforts to promote and organize events that have been linked to violence and extremism, and has pleaded the Fifth Amendment when questioned about his activities. Overall, Kirk's character and behavior are marked by a disregard for truth, a lack of empathy, and a willingness to promote and engage in harmful and discriminatory behavior.

entries.fact: Charlie Kirk said that a slot at the U.S. Military Academy in West Point, New York, that he considered his went to 'a far less-qualified candidate of a different gender and a different persuasion'.
Charlie Kirk said he would vote for Donald Trump despite not being a big fan, and that Trump's candidacy made Turning Point's mission more difficult
Charlie Kirk promoted debunked claims about George Floyd, such as that he was 'illegally counterfeiting currency' and had once 'put a gun to a pregnant woman's stomach'.

Charlie Kirk claimed he never said that a slot at the U.S. Military Academy in West Point, New York, that he considered his went to 'a far less-qualified candidate of a different gender and a different persuasion'.
Charlie Kirk promoted false allegations of electoral fraud in the 2020 election.
Charlie Kirk stated that Democrats shutting down college campuses would remove opportunities for ballot harvesting and voter fraud, and encouraged keeping campuses closed as it would be beneficial
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Turning Point Action and Students for Trump were sending more than 80 'buses of patriots to D.C. to fight for this president'
Charlie Kirk's organizations received $1.25 million from Publix heiress Julie Fancelli to fund buses to the January 6 event
Charlie Kirk paid $60,000 for Kimberly Guilfoyle to speak at the rally
Charlie Kirk called for a 'patriot' to bail out of jail the man who broke into Nancy Pelosi's house and attacked and tried to murder her husband with a hammer
Charlie Kirk pleaded the Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination while appearing before the U.S. House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment or the death penalty of Joe Biden for 'crimes against America'.

A Brookings Institution study found Kirk's podcast contained the second-highest proportion of false, misleading, and unsubstantiated statements among 36,603 episodes produced by 79 prominent political podcasters
Charlie Kirk stated that it should be legal to burn a rainbow or Black Lives Matter flag in public.
Charlie Kirk discussed the burning of pride flags and said that convictions for those arrested for the 'hate crime' of doing donuts over Pride flags painted on public streets should be overturned.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed on social media that the medical examiner who performed the autopsy declared George Floyd had died of an overdose.
Charlie Kirk deleted a tweet about human trafficking arrests after a fact-checker pointed out that the false 2018 number had originated on the conspiracy site 8chan
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that protesters in the French yellow vests movement chanted 'We want Trump'
Charlie Kirk spread falsehoods about voter fraud ahead of the 2020 U.S. presidential election
Charlie Kirk spread false information and conspiracy theories about COVID-19 on social media
Charlie Kirk alleged that the WHO covered up information about the COVID-19 pandemic
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that hydroxychloroquine had proved to be '100% effective in treating the virus'
Charlie Kirk falsely stated that during the 2009 swine flu pandemic it 'took President Barack Obama millions infected and over 1,000 deaths' to declare a public health emergency
Charlie Kirk described the public health measure of social distancing prohibitions in churches as a Democratic plot against Christianity
Charlie Kirk made the unfounded assertion that authorities in Wuhan, China, were burning patients
Charlie Kirk said he refused to abide by mask requirements because 'the science around masks is very questionable'
Charlie Kirk led a Stop the Steal protest at the Maricopa Tabulation Center in Phoenix
Charlie Kirk promoted false and disproven claims of fraud in the 2020 election
Charlie Kirk promoted misleading claims about the efficacy and safety of COVID-19 vaccines
Charlie Kirk called mandatory requirements for students to take the COVID-19 vaccine 'medical apartheid'
Charlie Kirk wrote an op-ed stating that 'the facts are that there are only two genders; that transgenderism and gender 'fluidity' are lies that hurt people and abuse kids'
Charlie Kirk called the separation of church and state in the United States a 'fabrication'
Charlie Kirk routinely made anti-LGBTQ remarks, opposing transgender rights and medical care, and asserted that there is an 'LGBTQ agenda'
Charlie Kirk claimed that being gay was an 'error' and likened homosexuality to drug addiction

Charlie Kirk criticized the Supreme Court's decision in Obergefell v. Hodges, calling LGBTQ activists the 'alphabet mafia' and claiming that the movement is not 'just about two dudes being able to get married'

Charlie Kirk said that 'all men are created equal in the eyes of God, all men and women, but not all cultures are created equal. To say that, you get attacked in every direction, but excuse me when I say that Western civilization is the best that humanity has produced. It's an outgrowth of the Bible'
Charlie Kirk said that transgender women in women's locker rooms should be 'taken care of the way we used to take care of things in the 1950s and '60s'

Charlie Kirk said, 'I think it's worth it, I think it's worth to have a cost of, unfortunately, some gun deaths every single year so that we can have the Second Amendment to protect our other God-given rights'

Charlie Kirk said, 'One of the reasons we're living through a constitutional crisis is that we no longer have a Christian nation, but we have a Christian form of government, and they're incompatible. You cannot have liberty if you do not have a Christian population'
Charlie Kirk said, 'This is a Christian state. I'd like to see it stay that way'

Charlie Kirk criticized YouTuber Ms. Rachel for celebrating Pride Month, quoting the Bible verse 'love thy neighbor' and arguing that she was being selective, while also referencing a verse that calls for the stoning of gay people
Charlie Kirk argued that abortion is murder and should be illegal, and opposed exceptions for rape, including for children as young as 10
Charlie Kirk compared abortion to the Holocaust, and said that abortion is worse

Charlie Kirk called for the elimination of Jasmine Crockett's congressional district, justifying the erasure of her district by claiming she was a part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'
Charlie Kirk asserted that the increasing presence of African Americans in American politics resulted in the Black community being 'worse off'.
Charlie Kirk criticized birth control, saying it creates 'very angry and bitter young ladies and young women'.

Charlie Kirk told followers of his radio podcast that Democratic immigration policies were aimed at 'diminishing and decreasing white demographics in America' and called for Texas to 'deputize a citizen force and put them on the border' to protect 'white demographics in America'.
Charlie Kirk told young women to go to college for the purpose of finding husbands and 'embrace their roles as mothers and homemakers'.

Charlie Kirk said that Democrats wanted Americans to live in a society with 'no cultural identity, where you live in sexual anarchy, where private property is a thing of the past, and the ruling class controls everything'.
Charlie Kirk said that the 'biblical model' for women to pursue in romantic relationships is a partner who is 'a protector and a leader, and deep down, a vast majority of you agree'.
Charlie Kirk called the transgender movement 'a throbbing middle finger to God' and 'against the natural law'.
Charlie Kirk said that 'prowling Blacks go around for fun to go target white people' in urban America.
Charlie Kirk reacted to Representative Sheila Jackson Lee's statement on affirmative action by saying, 'You do not have the brain processing power to otherwise be taken really seriously.'
Charlie Kirk described Martin Luther King Jr. as 'awful ... not a good person' and as someone who is admired only because he said 'one thing he didn't actually believe'.
Charlie Kirk condemned the Civil Rights Act of 1964, calling its passage a 'huge mistake' and alleging that it had created a 'permanent DEI-type bureaucracy'.
Charlie Kirk called for a nationwide ban on gender-affirming care for transgender people and demanded 'Nuremberg-style' trials for doctors who perform such care.
Charlie Kirk said, 'The great replacement strategy, which is well under way every single day in our southern border, is a strategy to replace white rural America with something different'.
Charlie Kirk said, 'The American Democrat party hates this country. They wanna see it collapse. They love it when America becomes less white.'

Charlie Kirk posted 'The ' Great Replacement ' is not a theory, it's a reality', alongside a Fox News headline that falsely claimed: '7.2M illegals entered the U.S. under Biden administration, an amount greater than population of 36 states.'
Charlie Kirk thought the Civil Rights Act of 1964 was a destructive force in American politics that had been turned into an anti-white weapon.

Charlie Kirk told The New York Times, 'I take the Caldwellian view, from his book The Age of Entitlement, that we went through a new founding in the '60s and that the Civil Rights Act has actually superseded the U.S. Constitution as its reference point.'

Charlie Kirk said that a 'myth' had been created around Martin Luther King Jr. which had 'grown totally out of control' and that King was currently 'the most honored, worshiped, even deified person of the 20th century' despite 'most people' supposedly disliking him during his life.
Charlie Kirk called Martin Luther King Jr. 'just a man ... a very flawed one at that' and a 'mythological anti-racist creation of the 1960s'.
Charlie Kirk blamed DEI programs for national aviation issues, saying, 'If I see a Black pilot, I'm going to be like, 'Boy, I hope he's qualified.'
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' and said she was nominated for the Supreme Court because of her race.

Charlie Kirk stated that 'America does not need more visas for people from India', arguing that the American workforce has become dominated by Indian-American immigrants, effectively decreasing job opportunities for Americans.
Charlie Kirk commented that America would still be America if it were ethnically 90% Indian, as long as they were Christian Indians.

Charlie Kirk elaborated on Hinduism and his disapproval of its morality due to its polytheism, stating: 'When you have multiple gods, you get different moralities. And the West has largely embraced the idea that there is a standard of conduct, or a best way to live.'
Charlie Kirk blamed the high death toll of the July 2025 Central Texas floods on DEI.

Charlie Kirk accused Democrats of spreading a 'false narrative' that 'there is a relentless assault against Black people on behalf of white people', and said 'White individuals are actually more likely to be attacked, especially even per capita, by Black individuals in this country.'
Charlie Kirk opposed the Paris Agreement on climate change
Charlie Kirk admitted that Turning Point USA had accepted funding from the fossil fuel industry and spoke out against targeting fossil fuels
Charlie Kirk was a speaker for the annual conference held by the anti-Muslim advocacy group ACT for America in Washington DC
Charlie Kirk claimed that there is no factual data to back up global warming and that scientists do not know the cause, which was later rated as inaccurate by Science Feedback
Charlie Kirk said that the southern border matters more than the Ukrainian border and called for Republican leaders to label the situation at the southern border an invasion
Charlie Kirk argued that it's not Islamophobia to notice that Muslims want to import values into the West that seek to destabilize our civilization.
Charlie Kirk said that immigration to the United States should be completely stopped.
Charlie Kirk called for the use of force against migrants at the U.S.–Mexico border, including the use of tear gas, rubber bullets, and whips.
Charlie Kirk called Mehdi Hasan to be deported and deplatformed over his views on the COVID-19 pandemic, calling him a 'neurotic lunatic'.
Charlie Kirk called U.S. representative Ilhan Omar a 'terrorist sympathizer' and called for her deportation.
Charlie Kirk blamed Hamas for the deaths of civilians in Gaza and denied that Israel is starving Palestinians.
Charlie Kirk said of Palestine, 'I don't think the place exists.'

Charlie Kirk said that 'Jewish donors have been the number 1 funding mechanism of radical, open border, neoliberal, quasi-Marxist policies ... This is a beast created by secular Jews, and now it's coming for Jews'.
Charlie Kirk suggested that secular Jews control 'not just the colleges; it's the nonprofits, it's the movies, it's Hollywood, it's all of it'.
Charlie Kirk said that 'Jews have been some of the largest funders of cultural Marxist ideas and supporters of those ideas over the last 30 or 40 years.'

Charlie Kirk promoted a conspiracy theory alleging the Israeli government knew that Hamas was going to launch the attack, and that Benjamin Netanyahu allowed it to go ahead as part of a plan to remain in power.
Charlie Kirk promoted the false claim that Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, were eating residents' pets and other wildlife.

Charlie Kirk offered an 'apology' to the Russian people, stating 'very few Americans want war with you' and that 'the people obsessed with fighting you forever' were a minority 'on their way out of power'.

Charlie Kirk responded to an inquiry about his claim not being inclusive of other religious worldviews, saying: 'I don't seek to be inclusive, I seek what is best. And the Ten Commandments are what is best. Would it be offensive to a young Hindu kid? Maybe, maybe not. But it also is a reminder they're living in a country that's a monotheistic country.'
Charlie Kirk texted associates that Jewish donors play into all the stereotypes and he felt pushed to leave the pro-Israel cause
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Islam is the sword the left is using to slit the throat of America
Charlie Kirk posted that 24 years ago a group of Muslims killed 2,753 people on 9/11, and now a Muslim Socialist is on pace to run New York City, which was described as a gross and Islamophobic post
Charlie Kirk called Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy a 'CIA puppet' and 'gangster' who 'sent his own people to a senseless massacre'.
Charlie Kirk stated that taking Taiwan would probably start a nuclear war and that the US should have taken Taiwan in 1950
Charlie Kirk opposed a bipartisan bill to expand anti-BDS laws, which punish the boycott of Israel.
Charlie Kirk said the bill would 'only create more antisemitism, and play into growing narratives that Israel is running the U.S. government'.
Charlie Kirk claimed that the philosophical foundation of anti-whiteness has been largely financed by Jewish donors
Charlie Kirk disagreed with Trump's decision to send more military aid to Ukraine, saying: 'We were against it with Biden. Why would we be for it now? Unless it gets us to a peace settlement'.
Charlie Kirk's 'Professor Watchlist' was criticized for targeting professors with death threats, forcing them off campus.
Charlie Kirk blamed Chicago gun violence on 'a lack of father problem in the Black community'
Charlie Kirk once said Jewish money was ruining US culture
Charlie Kirk shouted 'Fake News' at a town hall questioner for asking about something he actually said
Charlie Kirk's organization, Turning Point USA, stoked vaccine resistance as COVID-19 surged again.
Charlie Kirk was shown in videos of closed-door sessions of leading conservative activists, where he said 'Be not afraid of the accusations that you're a voter suppressor'

Charlie Kirk deleted a tweet saying he sent '80+ buses full of patriots' to D.C., two days before Trump supporters stormed the Capitol in an attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 presidential election.
Charlie Kirk said the Capitol attack was not an insurrection after deleting his '80+ buses' tweet.
Charlie Kirk warned that climate activism would erode American sovereignty and private property, describing it as a Trojan Horse for Marxism and likening it to pseudo-paganism
Charlie Kirk said on his podcast that the separation of church and state is 'a fabrication', 'a fiction', and 'not in the Constitution'.
Charlie Kirk stated that Joe Biden should be 'put in prison and/or given the death penalty for crimes against America'
Charlie Kirk used his platforms to decry racial equity programs, float conspiracy theories, and test divisive messaging, according to a long-time GOP consultant
Charlie Kirk promoted Christian Nationalism and far-right politically conservative politics through his organization TPUSA
Charlie Kirk pushed conspiracies about election fraud, Christian nationalism, anti-immigrant xenophobia, and racism, including attacks on Martin Luther King
Charlie Kirk once called for a 'patriot' to bail out Paul Pelosi assailant David DePape.
Charlie Kirk expressed views on gun control and used derogatory language, such as 'prowling blacks', in his own words
Charlie Kirk floated a conspiracy theory that Netanyahu issued a "stand down" order as a means to bolster his own political standing after the October 7th attacks.
Charlie Kirk's Twitter account was locked for spreading misinformation about mail-in votes
Charlie Kirk continued to push voter-fraud theories after the Electoral College confirmed Biden's victory
Charlie Kirk and Candace Owens falsely claimed there is no evidence of global warming and scientists don't know the cause in a Turning Point USA video
Charlie Kirk expressed a desire to start a border militia to protect 'White demographics in America'
Charlie Kirk referred to George Floyd as a 'scumbag'
Charlie Kirk said Democrats want Americans to live in 'sexual anarchy'
Charlie Kirk warned on YouTube that gay people "want to corrupt your children"
Charlie Kirk said Jewish money was ruining US culture
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' who is 'unqualified' for the Supreme Court
Charlie Kirk defended Elon Musk's antisemitism, stating 'Some of the largest financiers of left-wing anti-white causes have been Jewish Americans'
Charlie Kirk planned to discredit Martin Luther King Jr. and the Civil Rights Act
Charlie Kirk flipped on Martin Luther King Jr., attacking the growing 'myth' around him
Charlie Kirk called for shooting and whipping migrants on the southern border, saying "If you enter, we have lethal force, and we're willing to use it."
Charlie Kirk said 'We must ban trans-affirming care — the entire country. Donald Trump needs to run on this issue'
Charlie Kirk said 'We need to have a Nuremberg-style trial for every gender-affirming clinic doctor'
Charlie Kirk amplified a since-debunked social media rumor about Haitian immigrants in Springfield, Ohio
Charlie Kirk's rhetoric about 'reclaiming the country for Christ' has grown more bold, earning him the label of Christian nationalist
Charlie Kirk stated that the death toll from Texas flooding would not have been as high if it wasn't for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiatives
Charlie Kirk accused Rep. Jasmine Crockett of being part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'
Charlie Kirk once said prominent Black women didn't have 'brain processing power' to be taken seriously
Charlie Kirk referred to 'prowling Blacks' and 'the great replacement strategy'
Charlie Kirk said that abortion is 'worse than holocaust'
Charlie Kirk once said he would want a 10-year-old daughter who was raped to deliver the baby
Charlie Kirk railed against transgender rights
Charlie Kirk said some gun deaths are 'worth it' in order to have the Second Amendment
Charlie Kirk said the Civil Rights Act 'created a beast, and that beast has now turned into an anti-white weapon'
Charlie Kirk likened concern about climate change to 'pseudo-paganism'
Charlie Kirk warned of Islamophobia after Mamdani's win, saying New York "is about to see 9/11 2.0"
Charlie Kirk stated "I don't love the idea of sending arms to Ukraine"
